messages.content: Hi! Can you write me a program in C using Farey's Fractions

Can you write me a C program incorporating Farey's fractions, using this definition of Farey's fractions: Farey fractions of level one are defined as a sequence (0/1, 1/1). This sequence is extended in level two to form a sequence (0/1, 1/2, 1/1) , and then the sequence 
(0/1, 1/3, 1/2, 2/3, 1/1) at level three, and the following sequences 
 (0/1, 1/4, 1/3, 1/2, 2/3, 3/4, 1/1) at level four, so that at each level "n", a new fraction 
(a+b)/(c+d) is inserted between two neighbor fractions a/c and b/d only if c+d>=n.

Your program output must match the following runs exactly:
For an input of "1":
(0/1, 1/1)
For an input of "2":
(0/1, 1/1)
(0/1, 1/2, 1/1)
For an input of "3":
(0/1, 1/1)
(0/1, 1/2, 1/1)
(0/1, 1/3, 1/2, 2/3, 1/1)

The key is to do this one digit at a time. Each argument and the result gets their own stack. Push the integers representing the digits of each input onto their own stack then iterate over the non-empty stacks summing the digits and keep track of the carry.

You will need to declare separate pointers for each stack and make sure that your support routines only work on the stack that is passed to them. Push the result of each sum onto a result stack. Finally, print the result stack. You do not need to error check the input numbers nor handle negative numbers.

Your program must match the following runs exactly:
For an input of "123 456":
579
For an input of "65536 65536":
131072
For an input of "6553600000000000000000000 6553600000000000000000000":
13107200000000000000000000
Your program should ask for two input numbers in the format of :  <operand one> <operand two>

The output should add two arbitrary size integers entered from the command line. The key is to do this one digit at a time. Each argument and the result gets their own stack. So that an input of: "123 456" would produce an output of "579"

Can you edit your code so that your program matches the following runs exactly:
For an input of “123 456”:
579
For an input of “65536 65536”:
131072
For an input of “6553600000000000000000000 6553600000000000000000000”:
13107200000000000000000000
No, can you actually make it so that an  input of "123" and "456" will produce an output of "579"
Your program should ask for an input from the command line in the format of : <operand one> <operand two>
Your program is not working. It keeps producing an error : "free(): double free detected in tcache 2"

You have to do the calculation one digit at a time so that the each argument and the result gets their own stack. Push the integers representing the digits of each input onto their own stack then iterate over the non-empty stacks summing the digits and keep track of the carry. You will need to declare separate pointers for each stack and make sure that your support routines only work on the stack that is passed to them.
you are inserting the numbers the wrong way. The stack should go in the reverse direction
Can you edit the code so that an input of 65536 and 65536 produces an output of 131072
Can you debug your program? It is not running

Can you fix your program so that an input of: 65536 65536
produces an output of 131072
Your program does not produce an output of 131072 for an input of 65536 and 65536

There is an error of an implicit declaration of function 'is_empty' 
can you fix that

Can you edit your code so that an input of : 6553600000000000000000000 and 6553600000000000000000000 
produces an output of : 13107200000000000000000000
You are inserting your stack the wrong way. Reverse it
Can you debug your program
I will give you a C code and can you edit it to provide specific outputs?
Sure, I can help you edit your C code. Please provide the code and specify the output you're expecting from it.
This is the program, familiarize yourself with it:
Please provide the C program code that you'd like me to familiarize with and specify the changes you need, and I'll be happy to help you with it.

Thank you for providing the program code. This C program implements a stack-based approach to perform the addition of two arbitrary size integers, which could be very large beyond the standard integer range. The program takes two numbers as input, uses linked lists (stacks) to store their digits in reverse order (least significant digit on the top), adds them digit by digit considering the carry, and then prints out the addition result by recursively printing and freeing the nodes in the result stack.

The result of the addition is printed in the correct order (from the most significant digit to the least significant digit) because the input numbers are loaded into the stack in reverse order.

Let me know how you’d like me to edit the code to produce the desired output, and what specific changes you're looking for.
Yes, can you edit the code so that an input of: 65536 and 65536 , will produce a sum and result of 131072.

Your code is having errors and warnings with "struct int_node *" 
can you fix the program so that it can compile

Your code does not compile, can you fix that 
it is having trouble with struct int_node

Can you edit the code so that if the user does not input two numbers, then the following output occurs: 
bigadd <operand one> <operand two>
